title: Eamonn Holmes Gets Sick and Goes to the Hospital
summary: Eamonn Holmes is a famous TV host who works on a channel called GB News. He got very sick last week and had to go to the hospital. Doctors found out he had a stroke, which is when blood stops going to part of the brain. This can make someone's face droop or cause trouble talking. GB News said Eamonn is getting better with treatment, and his friends at work hope he recovers soon. He is 66 years old and has been on TV for many years, hosting shows like This Morning and a breakfast program. While he is in the hospital, another host named Alex Armstrong will take his place on the show. Eamonn has had other health problems before, like surgery on his back and hips. Everyone is sending good wishes to him.

background_read: Eamonn Holmes is a well-known broadcaster from Northern Ireland who has been on TV for decades, hosting shows like This Morning and breakfast programs. He recently joined GB News and has faced health issues, including spinal surgery and hip replacements. A stroke is a serious medical condition that requires quick help, and symptoms can include face drooping or speech problems. His colleagues are supporting him during his recovery.
Article_Structure: WHO: Eamonn Holmes, a 66-year-old TV host

WHERE: At a hospital, and he works for GB News
WHY: To recover from his illness, with colleagues wishing him well
